Elevated IL-6 levels associated colorectal cancer risk observational studies, associations may confounded lifestyle factors, adiposity, reverse causation (cancer causing inflammation rather vice versa). IL6R missense variant rs2228145 (Asp358Ala) biologically well-characterized perturbation pathway: increases soluble IL-6 receptor levels alters downstream signaling (Ferreira et al., 2013). Mendelian Randomization can address question: genetically proxied IL-6 signaling causally affect colorectal cancer risk? IL-6 receptor (IL6R) gene region chromosome 1q21 contains well-characterized variants (notably rs2228145/Asp358Ala) alter IL-6 signaling. serve strong genetic instruments MR analysis. Recent MR work circulating interleukins colorectal cancer established clear causal signal IL-6/IL-6 receptor traits, vignette read realistic workflow example anchored real scientific question, claim synthetic estimate reproduces published effect size (“Circulating interleukins risk colorectal cancer: Mendelian randomization study”, 2023).

text: keep vignette fully executable, simulate local site dataset preserving IL6R score definition used instrument table . avoids common MR mistake: fitting outcome model one score computing Wald ratio different denominator.
code: # Simulate one site using the same allele-score definition that Medusa fits set.seed(2026) n <- 8000  snpMatrix <- sapply(instruments$eaf, function(maf) rbinom(n, 2, maf)) colnames(snpMatrix) <- paste0("snp_", gsub("[^a-zA-Z0-9]", "_", instruments$snp_id))  scoreWeights <- instruments$beta_ZX / (instruments$se_ZX^2) scoreWeights <- scoreWeights / sum(abs(scoreWeights)) scoreBetaZX <- sum(scoreWeights * instruments$beta_ZX)  confounder1 <- rnorm(n) confounder2 <- rbinom(n, 1, 0.5)  exposure <- scoreBetaZX * as.numeric(snpMatrix %*% scoreWeights) +   0.3 * confounder1 + 0.3 * confounder2 + rnorm(n)  trueEffect <- -0.16 logOdds <- trueEffect * exposure + 0.3 * confounder1 + 0.3 * confounder2 outcome <- rbinom(n, 1, 1 / (1 + exp(-logOdds)))  cohortData <- data.frame(   person_id = seq_len(n),   outcome = outcome,   snpMatrix,   confounder_1 = confounder1,   confounder_2 = confounder2,   stringsAsFactors = FALSE )  betaGrid <- seq(-2, 2, by = 0.02)  # Fit the site-specific outcome model profile <- fitOutcomeModel(   cohortData = cohortData,   covariateData = NULL,   instrumentTable = instruments,   betaGrid = betaGrid,   siteId = "site_A" ) #> Fitting outcome model at site 'site_A' (4226 cases, 3774 controls)... #> Site 'site_A': beta_ZY_hat = -0.0766 (SE = 0.0749).

Create synthetic covariates and add one deliberately SNP-correlated trait diagnosticCovariates <- simulateCovariateData(   n = n,   nCovariates = 8,   seed = 2026 ) names(diagnosticCovariates)[names(diagnosticCovariates) == "person_id"] <- "personId" diagnosticCovariates$proxy_inflammation_trait <-   as.numeric(scale(diagnosticCohort$snp_rs2228145 + rnorm(n, sd = 0.05)))  diagnostics <- suppressWarnings(   runInstrumentDiagnostics(     cohortData = diagnosticCohort,     covariateData = diagnosticCovariates,     instrumentTable = instruments,     exposureProxyConceptIds = 1L,     pValueThreshold = 1e-4   ) ) #> Running instrument diagnostics for 7 SNPs... #>   Computing F-statistics... #>   Running instrument PheWAS... #>   PheWAS: tested 63 of 63 SNP-covariate pairs (0 skipped due to low N or no variation). #>   Comparing allele frequencies... #>   Summarizing genotype missingness... #> Instrument diagnostics complete.  # Assumption 1: relevance print(diagnostics$fStatistics[, c("snp_id", "fStatistic", "source", "weakFlag")]) #>       snp_id fStatistic      source weakFlag #> 1  rs2228145 16.7154796 cohort_data    FALSE #> 2  rs4129267 28.3757255 cohort_data    FALSE #> 3  rs7529229 11.8867337 cohort_data    FALSE #> 4  rs4845625  6.0412963 cohort_data     TRUE #> 5  rs6689306  0.2330404 cohort_data     TRUE #> 6 rs12118721  2.8435524 cohort_data     TRUE #> 7  rs4453032  0.2413660 cohort_data     TRUE  # text: illustrate federated pooling without requiring three live data partners, retain observed site_A profile create two additional site profiles score definition slightly different information content.
code: makeSyntheticSite <- function(baseProfile, siteId, infoScale, betaShift, caseScale) {   peak <- baseProfile$betaHat + betaShift   info <- (1 / (baseProfile$seHat^2)) * infoScale   logLik <- -0.5 * info * (baseProfile$betaGrid - peak)^2   logLik <- logLik - max(logLik)    synthetic <- list(     siteId = siteId,     betaGrid = baseProfile$betaGrid,     logLikProfile = logLik,     nCases = max(50L, as.integer(round(baseProfile$nCases * caseScale))),     nControls = max(50L, as.integer(round(baseProfile$nControls * caseScale))),     snpIds = baseProfile$snpIds,     diagnosticFlags = baseProfile$diagnosticFlags,     betaHat = peak,     seHat = sqrt(1 / info),     scoreDefinition = baseProfile$scoreDefinition   )   class(synthetic) <- "medusaSiteProfile"   synthetic }  siteProfiles <- list(   site_A = profile,   site_B = makeSyntheticSite(profile, "site_B", infoScale = 0.9,                              betaShift = 0.01, caseScale = 0.85),   site_C = makeSyntheticSite(profile, "site_C", infoScale = 1.1,                              betaShift = -0.01, caseScale = 1.15) )  # Pool combined <- poolLikelihoodProfiles(siteProfiles) #> Pooling profile likelihoods from 3 site(s)... #> Pooling complete: 3 sites, 12678 total cases, 11322 total controls.  # Visualize plotLikelihoodProfile(   combinedProfile = combined,   siteProfileList = siteProfiles,   title = "Profile Likelihood: IL-6 Signaling on Colorectal Cancer" )

text: Send script participating site, customized local connection details:
code: # ============================================================ # Medusa Site Analysis Script # Study: [YOUR STUDY NAME] # Site: [SITE NAME] # Date: [DATE] # ============================================================  library(Medusa)  # ----- Site-specific configuration ----- connectionDetails <- DatabaseConnector::createConnectionDetails(   dbms = "postgresql",              # Change to your DBMS   server = "localhost/ohdsi",       # Change to your server   user = "ohdsi_user",             # Change to your user   password = keyring::key_get("ohdsi_db")  # Use secure credential storage )  cdmDatabaseSchema <- "cdm"           # Change to your CDM schema cohortDatabaseSchema <- "results"     # Change to your results schema cohortTable <- "cohort"               # Change if different outcomeCohortId <- 1234               # Provided by coordinator genomicDatabaseSchema <- "genomics"   # Schema with VARIANT_OCCURRENCE (default: cdmDatabaseSchema) siteId <- "site_A"                    # Unique identifier for this site  # ----- Load instrument table (provided by coordinator) ----- instrumentTable <- read.csv("instruments.csv", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)  # ----- Step 1: Build cohort ----- cohortData <- buildMRCohort(   connectionDetails = connectionDetails,   cdmDatabaseSchema = cdmDatabaseSchema,   cohortDatabaseSchema = cohortDatabaseSchema,   cohortTable = cohortTable,   outcomeCohortId = outcomeCohortId,   instrumentTable = instrumentTable,   genomicDatabaseSchema = genomicDatabaseSchema,   washoutPeriod = 365,   excludePriorOutcome = TRUE )  # ----- Step 2: Build covariates ----- covariateData <- buildMRCovariates(   connectionDetails = connectionDetails,   cdmDatabaseSchema = cdmDatabaseSchema,   cohortDatabaseSchema = cohortDatabaseSchema,   cohortTable = cohortTable,   outcomeCohortId = outcomeCohortId )  # ----- Step 3: Run diagnostics ----- diagnostics <- runInstrumentDiagnostics(   cohortData = cohortData,   covariateData = covariateData,   instrumentTable = instrumentTable )  # ----- Step 4: Fit outcome model ----- # Use the SAME betaGrid as all other sites betaGrid <- seq(-3, 3, by = 0.01)  profile <- fitOutcomeModel(   cohortData = cohortData,   covariateData = covariateData,   instrumentTable = instrumentTable,   betaGrid = betaGrid,   siteId = siteId,   analysisType = "alleleScore" )  # ----- Step 5: Export and share ----- # Required for the primary federated MR estimate: exportSiteProfile(profile, outputDir = ".", prefix = "medusa")  # Optional: share a one-line-per-check summary of the richer diagnostics object. utils::write.csv(   data.frame(     check = names(diagnostics$diagnosticFlags),     flag = unname(diagnostics$diagnosticFlags),     stringsAsFactors = FALSE   ),   sprintf("medusa_diagnostic_flags_%s.csv", siteId),   row.names = FALSE )  # Optional: only run this block if the coordinator requested per-SNP summaries # for IVW / MR-Egger / weighted-median sensitivity analyses. profilePerSnp <- fitOutcomeModel(   cohortData = cohortData,   covariateData = covariateData,   instrumentTable = instrumentTable,   betaGrid = betaGrid,   siteId = siteId,   analysisType = "perSNP" ) utils::write.csv(   profilePerSnp$perSnpEstimates,   sprintf("medusa_per_snp_%s.csv", siteId),   row.names = FALSE )  message("Analysis complete. Share the required profile CSVs and, if requested, the per-SNP summary CSV.")

text: collecting profile CSV files sites:
code: library(Medusa)  # Load instrument table instrumentTable <- read.csv("instruments.csv", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)  # Import site profiles from CSV files siteProfiles <- list(   site_A = importSiteProfile("medusa_profile_site_A.csv"),   site_B = importSiteProfile("medusa_profile_site_B.csv"),   site_C = importSiteProfile("medusa_profile_site_C.csv") )  # Pool combined <- poolLikelihoodProfiles(siteProfiles)  # Estimate estimate <- computeMREstimate(combined, instrumentTable)  # Optional: collect simple diagnostic flag summaries if sites shared them. diagnosticFlagPaths <- c(   "medusa_diagnostic_flags_site_A.csv",   "medusa_diagnostic_flags_site_B.csv",   "medusa_diagnostic_flags_site_C.csv" ) if (all(file.exists(diagnosticFlagPaths))) {   siteDiagnosticFlags <- lapply(diagnosticFlagPaths, read.csv, stringsAsFactors = FALSE) }  # Optional: set this to NULL unless per-SNP CSVs were collected. sensitivity <- NULL  # Optional: fixed-effect pool the per-SNP summaries before sensitivity analyses. # Each site's per-SNP CSV should contain one row per SNP with beta_ZY and se_ZY. perSnpPaths <- c(   site_A = "medusa_per_snp_site_A.csv",   site_B = "medusa_per_snp_site_B.csv",   site_C = "medusa_per_snp_site_C.csv" ) if (all(file.exists(perSnpPaths))) {   perSnpBySite <- lapply(perSnpPaths, read.csv, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)    stackedPerSnp <- do.call(rbind, perSnpBySite)   splitPerSnp <- split(stackedPerSnp, stackedPerSnp$snp_id)    perSnpPooled <- do.call(     rbind,     lapply(splitPerSnp, function(df) {       weights <- 1 / (df$se_ZY^2)       data.frame(         snp_id = df$snp_id[1],         effect_allele = df$effect_allele[1],         other_allele = df$other_allele[1],         eaf = df$eaf[1],         beta_ZY = sum(weights * df$beta_ZY) / sum(weights),         se_ZY = sqrt(1 / sum(weights)),         beta_ZX = df$beta_ZX[1],         se_ZX = df$se_ZX[1],         pval_ZX = df$pval_ZX[1],         stringsAsFactors = FALSE       )     })   )    sensitivity <- runSensitivityAnalyses(     perSnpPooled,     methods = c("IVW", "MREgger", "WeightedMedian", "LeaveOneOut"),     engine = "internal"   ) }  # Report generateMRReport(   mrEstimate = estimate,   sensitivityResults = sensitivity,   combinedProfile = combined,   siteProfileList = siteProfiles,   instrumentTable = instrumentTable,   exposureLabel = "IL-6 signaling",   outcomeLabel = "Colorectal cancer" )

title: Getting Started with Medusa
text: Medusa (Mendelian Estimation Distributed Standardized Analytics) R package implements two-sample Mendelian Randomization (MR) within OHDSI ecosystem using OMOP Common Data Model. Mendelian Randomization uses genetic variants instrumental variables estimate causal effects exposures outcomes. genetic variants randomly allocated conception (like natural randomized trial), MR can provide evidence causality observational data. Two-sample MR separates analysis two parts: SNP-exposure associations (beta_ZX): Obtained published GWAS (typically large biobank studies) SNP-outcome associations (beta_ZY): Estimated cohort data causal estimate Wald ratio: beta_MR = beta_ZY / beta_ZX. vignette runnable sandbox: every example uses synthetic data, goal show workflow shape outputs rather make scientific claim.

title: Getting Started with Medusa
code: # Create synthetic partner sites that reuse the same allele-score definition. # This keeps the pooled likelihood compatible with the denominator used later # in computeMREstimate(). makePartnerProfile <- function(template, siteId, shift, infoScale, nCases, nControls) {   partner <- template   partner$siteId <- siteId   partner$betaHat <- template$betaHat + shift   partner$seHat <- template$seHat / sqrt(infoScale)   partner$logLikProfile <- -0.5 * infoScale *     ((template$betaGrid - partner$betaHat) / template$seHat)^2   partner$logLikProfile <- partner$logLikProfile - max(partner$logLikProfile)   partner$nCases <- nCases   partner$nControls <- nControls   partner }  profiles <- list(   site_A = profileSiteA,   site_B = makePartnerProfile(profileSiteA, "site_B", shift = 0.06,                               infoScale = 1.1, nCases = 295, nControls = 2705),   site_C = makePartnerProfile(profileSiteA, "site_C", shift = -0.04,                               infoScale = 0.9, nCases = 260, nControls = 2740) )  # Pool the profiles combined <- poolLikelihoodProfiles(profiles) #> Pooling profile likelihoods from 3 site(s)... #> Pooling complete: 3 sites, 4007 total cases, 6993 total controls. cat(sprintf("Pooled %d sites: %d total cases, %d total controls\n",             combined$nSites, combined$totalCases, combined$totalControls)) #> Pooled 3 sites: 4007 total cases, 6993 total controls

what: Medusa: Mendelian Estimation in Distributed Standardized Analytics — Medusa-package
title: Medusa: Mendelian Estimation in Distributed Standardized Analytics — Medusa-package
text: Medusa implements two-sample Mendelian Randomization (MR) within OHDSI ecosystem using OMOP Common Data Model data substrate. package enables federated causal inference across distributed health networks without requiring individual-level data leave site. core methodological innovation one-shot federated pooling via profile likelihood aggregation: site computes log-likelihood profile across grid parameter values shares vector numbers. coordinator sums profiles across sites obtain pooled estimate without iterative communication protocol.

title: Medusa: Mendelian Estimation in Distributed Standardized Analytics — Medusa-package
text: analysis pipeline consists nine modules: Instrument Assembly (getMRInstruments):     Query OpenGWAS GWAS summary statistics apply LD clumping. Cohort Extraction (buildMRCohort):     Extract outcome cohorts genotype data OMOP CDM sites. Covariate Assembly (buildMRCovariates):     Assemble covariates via FeatureExtraction adjustment diagnostics. Instrument Diagnostics (runInstrumentDiagnostics):     Validate instruments via F-statistics, PheWAS, allele-frequency checks,     missingness summaries, placeholder negative-control interface. Outcome Model (fitOutcomeModel):     Fit binary outcome model evaluate exact penalized profile     log-likelihood grid. Likelihood Pooling (poolLikelihoodProfiles):     Aggregate site-level log-likelihood profiles via pointwise summation. MR Estimation (computeMREstimate):     Compute Wald ratio estimate delta method standard errors. Sensitivity Analyses (runSensitivityAnalyses):     IVW, MR-Egger, weighted median, Steiger filtering, leave-one-. Reporting (generateMRReport):     Generate self-contained HTML report results diagnostics.

text: Runs locally OMOP CDM site. Executes parameterized SQL extract outcome cohort data genotype data, performs allele harmonization ensure genotype coding matches instrument table, returns local R data frame suitable downstream analysis. returned data never leaves site. Genotype data extracted VARIANT_OCCURRENCE table defined OMOP CDM Genomic Extension. minimal required columns table : person_id — links variants persons rs_id — dbSNP rs identifier variant genotype — genotype call (VCF-style "0/0", "0/1", "1/1"     plain integer "0", "1", "2") Additionally, reference_allele alternate_allele used allele harmonization available. function also queries: PERSON (age, sex), CONDITION_OCCURRENCE (outcome), OBSERVATION_PERIOD (eligibility). SQL rendered translated via SqlRender cross-dialect compatibility.

what: Wald ratio MR estimate from pooled profile likelihood — computeMREstimate
title: Wald ratio MR estimate from pooled profile likelihood — computeMREstimate
text: Takes pooled profile log-likelihood poolLikelihoodProfiles instrument table compute MR causal estimate via Wald ratio. SNP-outcome association (beta_ZY) estimated profile likelihood peak, causal estimate beta_MR = beta_ZY / beta_ZX. multi-SNP analyses, beta_ZX association exact weighted allele score used site, simple mean component SNP effects. Uncertainty propagated using delta method. Confidence intervals computed profile likelihood (likelihood ratio method) via delta method (normal approximation). likelihood- based CI preferred assume normality.

title: Wald ratio MR estimate from pooled profile likelihood — computeMREstimate
code: computeMREstimate(combinedProfile, instrumentTable, ciLevel = 0.95)

title: Wald ratio MR estimate from pooled profile likelihood — computeMREstimate
text: combinedProfile Output poolLikelihoodProfiles. instrumentTable Data frame. Output getMRInstruments. Used fallback beta_ZX se_ZX values pooled profile carry stored score definition. ciLevel Numeric. Confidence interval level. Default 0.95.

title: Wald ratio MR estimate from pooled profile likelihood — computeMREstimate
text: list class "medusaMREstimate" containing: betaZY Point estimate SNP-outcome association (profile MLE). seZY Standard error betaZY profile curvature. betaMR Causal effect estimate: betaZY / betaZX. seMR Standard error betaMR via delta method. ciLower Lower confidence limit betaMR. ciUpper Upper confidence limit betaMR. pValue Two-sided p-value betaMR. oddsRatio Exp(betaMR) — causal odds ratio binary outcomes. orCiLower Lower CI odds ratio. orCiUpper Upper CI odds ratio. ciLevel confidence level used. betaZX exposure effect fitted allele score. seZX SE betaZX used. nInstruments Number instruments used. combinedProfile full combined profile plotting.

title: Wald ratio MR estimate from pooled profile likelihood — computeMREstimate
text: Compute Mendelian Randomization Causal Estimate Wald ratio: single instrument, beta_MR = beta_ZY / beta_ZX. multiple instruments pooled single allele score, denominator must exposure effect score: $$\beta_{ZX, score} = \sum_j w_j \gamma_j$$ \(w_j\) fixed score weights used sites \(\gamma_j\) SNP-exposure coefficients. Delta method SE: se_MR = sqrt( (se_ZY/beta_ZX)^2 + (beta_ZY * se_ZX / beta_ZX^2)^2 ). Likelihood-based CI: region betaZY values log-likelihood drop peak - qchisq(ciLevel, df=1)/2. CI transformed MR scale via division beta_ZX.

title: Wald ratio MR estimate from pooled profile likelihood — computeMREstimate

text: Fits logistic regression binary outcome SNP genotype(s) plus covariates, evaluates profile log-likelihood across pre-specified grid beta_ZY values. core methodological function federated MR: site runs locally shares resulting log-likelihood profile vector. title: Outcome model with exact grid likelihood evaluation — fitOutcomeModel
text: cohortData Data frame. Output buildMRCohort. covariateData Covariate data. Output buildMRCovariates data frame person_id covariate columns. instrumentTable Data frame. Output getMRInstruments. betaGrid Numeric vector. Grid beta_ZY values evaluate profile log-likelihood. Default seq(-3, 3, = 0.01) (601 grid points). regularizationVariance Numeric. modelBackend = "cyclops", variance normal prior applied nuisance coefficients. Smaller values imply stronger shrinkage; use Inf unpenalized Cyclops fit. Ignored "glm" backend. Default 0.1. instrumentRegularization Logical. modelBackend = "cyclops", whether exposure coefficient included Cyclops prior. Default FALSE profiled exposure coefficient remains unpenalized. Ignored "glm" backend. outcomeType Character. Type outcome: "binary" logistic regression. Default "binary". analysisType Character. "alleleScore" single weighted score model "perSNP" separate per-SNP models. Default "alleleScore". siteId Character. Identifier site. Included returned profile object. Default "site_1". modelBackend Character. Outcome-model fitting backend: "glm" uses base R logistic regression, "cyclops" uses Cyclops scalable logistic regression optional Gaussian shrinkage nuisance covariates. Default "glm".

title: Outcome model with exact grid likelihood evaluation — fitOutcomeModel
text: Fit Outcome Model Evaluate Profile Log-Likelihood profile log-likelihood evaluation works follows: Fit unconstrained model obtain MLE Wald SE. grid point, fix beta_ZY value re-fit     nuisance parameters using fixed term offset. Record maximized constrained log-likelihood grid point.     exact profile likelihood coefficient logistic     generalized linear model. modelBackend = "cyclops" regularizationVariance finite, nuisance parameters estimated Gaussian prior unconstrained optimum every grid point. configuration returned objective penalized profile, unpenalized MLE profile. using allele score, weights \(w_j = \gamma_j / \mathrm{SE}(\gamma_j)^2\), normalized \(\sum_j |w_j|\). weights reused downstream MR denominator matches exact score fitted site. Missing SNP dosages allele-score model imputed expected dosage \(2 \times \mathrm{EAF}_j\) instrument table, rather treated homozygous reference. avoids systematic downward bias score genotype missingness present.

what: Allele harmonization for Mendelian Randomization — harmonizeAlleles
title: Allele harmonization for Mendelian Randomization — harmonizeAlleles
text: Ensures effect allele coding instrument table (GWAS) matches allele coding genotype data site. Handles allele flipping (alleles swapped) detects strand-ambiguous SNPs (/T G/C pairs) reliably harmonized. alleles need flipped, beta_ZX multiplied -1 EAF replaced 1 - EAF. Palindromic SNPs EAF near 0.5 (within eafPalindromicThreshold 0.5) dropped strand reliably inferred allele frequency.

title: Allele harmonization for Mendelian Randomization — harmonizeAlleles
text: instrumentTable Data frame columns: snp_id, effect_allele, other_allele, beta_ZX, se_ZX, eaf. Typically output getMRInstruments. genotypeAlleles Data frame columns: snp_id, allele_coded, allele_noncoded. Describes allele coding used genotype data particular site. eafPalindromicThreshold Numeric threshold removing palindromic SNPs. Palindromic SNPs EAF (0.5 - threshold) (0.5 + threshold) removed. Default 0.08 (.e., EAF 0.42 0.58). cohortAlleleFrequencies Optional named numeric vector cohort allele frequencies, keyed SNP ID. frequency coded allele genotype data (.e., mean(genotype)/2). Used resolve palindromic SNPs EAF far enough 0.5.

title: Allele harmonization for Mendelian Randomization — harmonizeAlleles
text: list two elements: instrumentTable harmonized instrument table updated       effect_allele, other_allele, beta_ZX, eaf allele flips       applied. logical column flipped added. removedSnps Data frame SNPs removed, column       reason indicating (e.g., "palindromic_ambiguous_eaf",       "allele_mismatch").

title: Allele harmonization for Mendelian Randomization — harmonizeAlleles
text: Harmonize Alleles Instrument Table Genotype Data harmonization algorithm proceeds follows SNP: instrument effect_allele matches genotype allele_coded:     action needed. instrument effect_allele matches genotype allele_noncoded:     flip coding (beta_ZX *= -1, eaf = 1 - eaf). neither direct match found, try complement alleles     (<->T, G<->C): SNP palindromic (/T G/C) EAF near 0.5,         remove SNP. palindromic EAF clearly differs 0.5, use EAF         infer strand orientation. match found even complements: remove SNP     reason "allele_mismatch".

title: Allele harmonization for Mendelian Randomization — harmonizeAlleles
text: Hartwig, F. P., Davies, N. M., Hemani, G., & Davey Smith, G. (2016). Two-sample Mendelian randomization: avoiding downsides powerful, widely applicable potentially fallible technique. International Journal Epidemiology, 45(6), 1717-1726.

title: Federated one-shot likelihood pooling — poolLikelihoodProfiles
text: Combines profile log-likelihood vectors multiple federated sites pointwise summation. core one-shot federated analysis: summing log-likelihoods equivalent multiplying likelihoods, yielding joint likelihood independence across sites. summing, function validates sites used identical beta grid vectors. grids differ, linear interpolation common grid overlapping beta range applied warning. Pooling never extrapolates beyond site's evaluated range.

title: Federated one-shot likelihood pooling — poolLikelihoodProfiles
text: Pool Profile Log-Likelihoods Across Sites pooling operation mathematically straightforward: grid point \(b_k\), combined log-likelihood $$\ell_{combined}(b_k) = \sum_{s=1}^{S} \ell_s(b_k)$$ \(\ell_s\) profile log-likelihood site \(s\). pooling exact assumption observations independent across sites every site uses beta grid. interpolation needed, pooling remains close numerical approximation common grid, beta range evaluated every site. iterative communication protocol needed: site computes profile shares numeric vector.

title: Instrument diagnostics for MR analysis — runInstrumentDiagnostics
text: Run Instrument Validation Diagnostics F-statistic: exposureProxyConceptIds provided, first-stage F-statistic computed regressing proxy measurement SNP. Otherwise, GWAS approximation (beta_ZX / se_ZX)^2 used. F < 10 indicates potentially weak instrument. PheWAS: SNP regressed every covariate covariate matrix using logistic (binary) linear (continuous) regression. Bonferroni correction applied. Significant associations may indicate pleiotropy. Allele frequency check: Compares effect allele frequency cohort GWAS reference. discrepancy > 0.1 may indicate strand flip population mismatch.

what: Standard Mendelian Randomization sensitivity analyses — runSensitivityAnalyses
title: Standard Mendelian Randomization sensitivity analyses — runSensitivityAnalyses
text: Implements multiple MR estimation methods robust different patterns instrument invalidity. Requires per-SNP beta_ZY estimates (available analysisType = "perSNP" fitOutcomeModel). Methods include: Inverse Variance Weighted (IVW), MR-Egger regression, Weighted Median, Steiger directional filtering, Leave-One-analysis. Concordance across methods strengthens causal evidence.

title: Standard Mendelian Randomization sensitivity analyses — runSensitivityAnalyses
code: runSensitivityAnalyses(   perSnpEstimates,   methods = c("IVW", "MREgger", "WeightedMedian", "Steiger", "LeaveOneOut"),   outcomeSampleSize = NULL,   exposureSampleSize = NULL,   outcomeType = "binary",   engine = "auto",   bootstrapSeed = 42 )

title: Standard Mendelian Randomization sensitivity analyses — runSensitivityAnalyses
text: perSnpEstimates Data frame per-SNP summary statistics. Required columns: snp_id, beta_ZY, se_ZY, beta_ZX, se_ZX, effect_allele, other_allele, eaf. allele fields make object harmonisation-ready delegation TwoSampleMR. Optional columns pval_ZX pval_ZY used present otherwise derived reported estimates. methods Character vector methods run. Default c("IVW", "MREgger", "WeightedMedian", "Steiger", "LeaveOneOut"). outcomeSampleSize Optional integer. Total sample size outcome GWAS/analysis. Needed Steiger filtering. exposureSampleSize Optional integer. Total sample size exposure GWAS. Needed Steiger filtering. outcomeType Character. Type outcome summary statistics represented perSnpEstimates. Medusa's internal Steiger implementation available "continuous" outcomes, engine = "TwoSampleMR" delegates TwoSampleMR's Steiger implementation harmonised data. Default "binary". engine Character. "auto" prefers TwoSampleMR installed otherwise falls back Medusa's internal implementations. Use "TwoSampleMR" require delegation, "internal" force Medusa's built-implementations. Default "auto". bootstrapSeed Integer NULL. Seed parametric bootstrap used internal Weighted Median SE estimator. Default 42, gives reproducible results. Set NULL use ambient RNG state.

title: Standard Mendelian Randomization sensitivity analyses — runSensitivityAnalyses
text: named list class "medusaSensitivity" containing: ivw Data frame method, beta_MR, se_MR, ci_lower, ci_upper, pval. mrEgger Data frame beta_MR, se_MR, pval, plus intercept,       intercept_se, intercept_pval. weightedMedian Data frame beta_MR, se_MR, ci_lower, ci_upper, pval. steiger Data frame IVW results Steiger filtering, plus       n_removed (number SNPs removed). leaveOneOut Data frame snp_removed, beta_MR, se_MR, pval       SNP dropped. summary Data frame comparing methods side side.

title: Standard Mendelian Randomization sensitivity analyses — runSensitivityAnalyses
text: Run MR Sensitivity Analyses IVW: Weighted regression beta_ZY beta_ZX origin, weights 1/se_ZY^2. primary MR estimate assuming instruments valid. MR-Egger: Weighted regression beta_ZY beta_ZX intercept, orienting SNPs beta_ZX positive. non-zero intercept indicates directional pleiotropy. slope consistent causal estimate even directional pleiotropy, provided InSIDE assumption holds. Weighted Median: Weighted median per-SNP Wald ratio estimates using first-order inverse-variance weights ratio estimates (\(w_j = \beta_{Xj}^2 / \mathrm{SE}(\beta_{Yj})^2\)). Produces consistent estimate fewer 50 Steiger: Tests whether SNP explains variance exposure outcome (expected causal direction). engine = "internal", Medusa applies continuous-trait correlation-based approximation described Hemani et al. (2017). engine = "TwoSampleMR", Medusa delegates TwoSampleMR::steiger_filtering() harmonised summary data. Leave-One-: Drops SNP turn recomputes IVW estimate. Identifies influential outlier instruments.

title: Standard Mendelian Randomization sensitivity analyses — runSensitivityAnalyses
text: Bowden, J., Davey Smith, G., & Burgess, S. (2015). Mendelian randomization invalid instruments: effect estimation bias detection Egger regression. International Journal Epidemiology, 44(2), 512-525. doi:10.1093/ije/dyv080. Abstract: https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/26050253/ Full text (available ): https://academic.oup.com/ije/article-pdf/44/2/512/18631079/dyv080.pdf Bowden, J., et al. (2016). Consistent estimation Mendelian randomization invalid instruments using weighted median estimator. Genetic Epidemiology, 40(4), 304-314. doi:10.1002/gepi.21965. Open access: https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC4849733/ Hemani, G., Tilling, K., & Davey Smith, G. (2017). Orienting causal relationship imprecisely measured traits using GWAS summary data. PLoS Genetics, 13(11), e1007081. doi:10.1371/journal.pgen.1007081. Open access: https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC5711033/

title: Standard Mendelian Randomization sensitivity analyses — runSensitivityAnalyses
code: # Simulate per-SNP estimates set.seed(42) nSnps <- 10 betaZX <- rnorm(nSnps, 0.3, 0.05) betaZY <- 0.5 * betaZX + rnorm(nSnps, 0, 0.02) perSnp <- data.frame(   snp_id = paste0("rs", 1:nSnps),   effect_allele = c("A", "C", "G", "T", "A", "C", "G", "T", "A", "C"),   other_allele = c("C", "G", "T", "A", "C", "G", "T", "A", "C", "G"),   eaf = seq(0.1, 0.55, length.out = nSnps),   beta_ZY = betaZY,   se_ZY = rep(0.02, nSnps),   beta_ZX = betaZX,   se_ZX = rep(0.05, nSnps) ) results <- runSensitivityAnalyses(   perSnp,   outcomeSampleSize = 10000,   exposureSampleSize = 10000,   outcomeType = "continuous" ) #> Running sensitivity analyses with 10 SNPs... #>   Engine: TwoSampleMR #> Harmonising exposure (medusa_exposure) and outcome (medusa_outcome) #> Removing the following SNPs for being palindromic with intermediate allele frequencies: #> rs10, rs8 #>   IVW... #> Analysing 'medusa_exposure' on 'medusa_outcome' #>   MR-Egger... #> Analysing 'medusa_exposure' on 'medusa_outcome' #>   Weighted Median... #> Analysing 'medusa_exposure' on 'medusa_outcome' #>   Steiger filtering... #> Analysing 'medusa_exposure' on 'medusa_outcome' #>   Leave-One-Out... #> Sensitivity analyses complete. results$summary #>                 method    beta_MR      se_MR   ci_lower  ci_upper         pval #> 1                  IVW 0.49183243 0.03090498  0.4312587 0.5524062 5.039535e-57 #> 2             MR-Egger 0.08879307 0.22182774 -0.3459893 0.5235754 7.028132e-01 #> 3      Weighted Median 0.48415523 0.04343351  0.3990256 0.5692849 7.402441e-29 #> 4 Steiger-filtered IVW 0.37825702 0.04988512  0.2804822 0.4760319 3.387952e-14
